summary: Changing "application/run/main_scene" in Project Settings is Bugged Slightly

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproduceable in: v4.4.stable.steam [4c311cbee]

### System information

Windows 11 (build 26100) - Vulkan (Forward+) - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - AMD Ryzen 9 5900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads)

### Issue description

There's a curious problem right now with the Godot editor specifically with changing the "application/run/main_scene" variable in the Project Settings. This is a little hard to explain so bear with me.

The text box for entering the string value for this setting in the Project Settings window is silently setting the actual variable back to whatever was in this text box when the Project Settings window was opened, but ONLY if the very next thing you do after changing this text string is hit enter to apply the change and then immediately close the Project Settings window while this text box still has the focus. So long as this text box is no longer in focus when closing the Project Settings window the new value for the variable is applied appropriately.

The message output offers some clues as to what might be going on: If you go to change this variable in the Project settings and hit enter, you get an output message "Set main_scene", however, upon closing the Project Settings window while this text entry still has the focus, you get a SECOND "Set main_scene". It's this second one which seems to be setting the value back to whatever was in the text box upon opening the Project Settings window.

And I'm saying all of this the way I am because there's clearly a disconnect between what the editor is reporting and the actual variable, since if this bug occurs and you go to the Project Settings again, the text box reports the new value you just put in, but if you now go to edit this value, keep it the same, apply it, and close the window, you still see "Set main_scene" twice but now the value has been applied correctly.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open Project Settings.
2. Change "application/run/main_scene" to something else and hit enter. You will see "Set main_scene" in the message output.
3. Immediately close the Project Settings window. You will see another "Set main_scene" in the message output.
4. Attempt to run the project. It will load into whichever scene was the main_scene prior to being changed.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

This can be tested with any project which has two or more different root scenes you can launch from.

summary: Add virtual joypad buttons for semantic "yes"/"no"

description.text:
This PR adds "virtual" joypad buttons which correspond to *semantic* inputs rather than *physical* inputs.

These virtual button events can be mapped to actions as usual, allowing interface actions such as "confirm" or "cancel" to use any supported controller's native semantics.

For example, although the `A` buttons on Xbox and Switch controllers send `JoyButton::A` and `JoyButton::B` respectively, both inputs would now also send `JoyButton::SEMANTIC_YES` as well.

Tested on macOS 15.3 with Switch Pro Controller and Switch Joy-Cons, needs testing on other platforms/controllers.

## Motivation

Previously, joypad face button events were identified strictly by position (ie top, bottom, left, right).
This is typically desirable for gameplay, as the control layout can be designed once and keep the same ergonomics across controllers.

However, this system created a problem when accounting for controller layouts whose face button semantics are reversed:
For example, Xbox controllers use the **bottom** (`A`) button for "yes," "confirm," or "select" in menu navigation, whereas Switch controllers use the **right** (`A`) button for these purposes.
Thus, mapping one of these face button positions to menu navigation is always unintuitive for some controllers.

The goal of this PR is to allow developers to easily implement semantically-correct interaction.
This is relevant to most games, so I believe it makes sense as an engine feature.

summary: Overall performance decreased on 4.4 from 4.3 on OSX M1 devices, especially in the Metal rendering backend

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.3 Stable, 4.4 Stable

### System information

Macbook Air M1

### Issue description

When I upgraded to 4.4, I noticed that my project (which, I should note, pushes the limits of my device's hardware already) was running significantly worse than previously. I've done some tests and have linked it to the new metal renderer. Some data I've collected:

Scene notes: PBR textures (trimsheet), bloom, LightmapGI, ReflectionProbes, Subviewport rendertargets, volumetric fog (0 density), directional light (does not affect models)
Same exact scene for all versions
Camera placed to observe both render targets
HighDPI enabled (actual resolution: 2560 × 1600)
No optimizations active
5 samples taken, each left running in fullscreen for 30 seconds

4.3 (Vulkan/MoltenVK only): ~54-56 FPS average 

4.4 (Vulkan fallback): ~52-43 FPS, minor stutters when moving camera

4.4 (Metal): ~ 49-50 FPS, major stutters when moving in and out of view of render targets

Prior to the 4.4 update I was able to maintain a stable 60FPS with 1 render target and the aforementioned settings. As of 4.4, neither backends can keep the FPS stable especially when in the game proper as opposed to this testing scene. I recognize that this is a lot of "graphical pizazz" especially for such a low-end device, but the fact that it _was_ able to maintain such a framerate and is no longer able to is my concern, ESPECIALLY with the metal backend which should (in theory) be _much_ faster on M1 macs. This issue also shows up in scenes with non-pbr textures. 

MRP NOTE: Due to how this issue is reproduced (requiring PBR textures) the MRP will not fit into the 10 MB limit. Thus, I have linked a github repo containing it. All tests were conducted by averaging 5 runs from the project selection menu, with duplicates of the project changing the respective version/backend.

Sorry if this is a poor/unusually formatted issue. Something in my brain makes me irrationally annoyed when I can't get my game running smoothly with some arbitrary level of graphical fidelity. This update as a whole seems to have a lot of issues on mac, so I'll probably make a couple issues for other stuff, but this is the main thing sticking out to me. Please tell me if there's anything I can do to make diagnosing this easier. <3



### Steps to reproduce

TESTING DONE ON SILICON MACBOOK AIR M1

Download the project and run it via the editor or the project selection menu. To do the variations, change the rendering backend, or open it in 4.3. It should run out of the box.

Reproduction in a normal project: Place rendertargets, PBR textures, and turn on bloom, vol. fog, etc. Then, simply run the scene.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

https://github.com/StrawberrySmoothieDev/Godot-4.4-Performance-MRP

summary: CollisionShape3D.debug_fill has much Z-fighting

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4-stable

### System information

Debian 12 (X11), Godot 4.4-stable, Vulkan (Forward+), RTX 3060

### Issue description

The new debug shape colors https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/90644 paired with "fill" has z-fighting.
Thats bad for level geometry.

`CollisionShape.debug_fill` is enabled by default.

The problem is that z-fighting happens when mesh and shape match in space, not sure if there's any solution for that.

For now would be better to disable `CollisionShape.debug_fill` by default.

summary: Font resolution wrong when setting `content_scale_factor` in `size_changed` signal handler

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee]

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Arch Linux #1 ZEN SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 27 Feb 2025 18:09:27 +0000 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated Intel(R) Arc(tm) Pro A60M Graphics (DG2) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900H (20 threads)

### Issue description

In my project I listen for changes of the main window size and scale its content by setting `get_window().content_scale_factor` accordingly.
I noticed that text in Labels (probably other text rendering Nodes too) gets blurry when making the Window larger (= increasing Viewport resolution) and increasing `content_scale_factor` in the `size_changed` event of the Window.

When you set `content_scale_factor` to the same value again after the `size_changed` event, text renders with the correct resolution again.

I was only able to replicate this inside `side_changed` signal handlers. Binding an input to change `content_scale_factor` does not result in this behavior for me.

Setting `content_scale_factor` with `set_deferred` also prevents this bug from happening.

### Steps to reproduce

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cde12293-86cd-44cc-98f0-8e7b85759423

In the video I first shrink and enlarge the Window again to reproduce the bug. Then I press Enter, which assigns `content_scale_factor` to the same value again and fixes the blurry text.

Not immediately obvious, it is only text that gets blurry, not the entire viewport. If you place a big Sprite2D with a high resolution texture behind the Labels, that Sprite2D is always rendered correctly.

```gdscript
func _ready() -> void:
	get_window().size_changed.connect(_on_window_size_changed)

func _on_window_size_changed():
	get_window().content_scale_factor = get_window().size.x / 1920.0

func _unhandled_input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
	if event.is_action_pressed("ui_accept"):
		get_window().content_scale_factor = get_window().content_scale_factor
```

summary: [4.4] Font importing (.ttf) gets stuck when trying to export with Godot

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.4.stable.mono.official.4c311cbee
- Not reproducible in: 4.3

### System information

Fedora 41 podman container, also on Fedora 41 GNOME desktop

### Issue description

There seems to be a new bug in Godot 4.4 where my game export process inside a container just hangs indefinitely when it tries to import some .ttf font files (I've left it be for over an hour with no new terminal output). I kept deleting the files as the process got stuck and after about 10 I stopped testing so I think this isn't specific to any font file (unless all NotoSans family fonts have the issue).

If I delete all of the .ttf font files before running `--export-release` then that makes the problem go away (i.e. the export process doesn't get stuck indefinitely and completes normally other than errors about the missing fonts).

Also what I found works is running `godot . --import --headless --quit-after 5` before the export. That was from some earlier [issues](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/99284) about the export process [being stuck](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/95287), but I personally didn't have to put those in my project for 4.3 as I didn't experience the getting stuck issue there. But now in 4.4 it seems like I do need the workaround.

Also to narrow things down I confirmed the same problem on my desktop outside the container environment and even running in non-headless mode. What I saw was that if I deleted the imported fonts from my .godot folder and opened the Godot Editor window to my project, that would forever hang like this as well:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9c8d95e9-9516-4e29-82b0-8e0497420600)

So yeah to my eyes this seems to be a general bug with font importing in Godot 4.4 where it for some reason gets stuck when it is depended on to complete.

### Steps to reproduce

Here is a `Dockerfile` which when run with `podman build` should show the issue of getting stuck:

```dockerfile
FROM fedora:41

RUN dnf install -y --setopt=deltarpm=false git git-lfs unzip wget dotnet-sdk-9.0 \
    gettext p7zip fontconfig && dnf clean all
RUN git lfs install

# Godot install
ENV GODOT_VERSION "4.4"

RUN wget https://github.com/godotengine/godot/releases/download/${GODOT_VERSION}-stable/Godot_v${GODOT_VERSION}-stable_mono_linux_x86_64.zip \
    && wget https://github.com/godotengine/godot/releases/download/${GODOT_VERSION}-stable/Godot_v${GODOT_VERSION}-stable_mono_export_templates.tpz \
    && mkdir .p ~/.cache \
    && mkdir -p ~/.config/godot \
    && mkdir -p ~/.local/share/godot/export_templates/${GODOT_VERSION}.stable.mono \
    && unzip Godot_v${GODOT_VERSION}-stable_mono_linux_x86_64.zip \
    && mv Godot_v${GODOT_VERSION}-stable_mono_linux_x86_64 godot_dir \
    && mv godot_dir/* /usr/local/bin/ \
    && ln -s /usr/local/bin/Godot_v${GODOT_VERSION}-stable_mono_linux.x86_64 /usr/local/bin/godot \
    && unzip Godot_v${GODOT_VERSION}-stable_mono_export_templates.tpz \
    && mkdir -p ~/.local/share/godot/export_templates/${GODOT_VERSION}.stable.mono \
    && mv templates/* ~/.local/share/godot/export_templates/${GODOT_VERSION}.stable.mono/ \
    && rm Godot_v${GODOT_VERSION}-stable_mono_export_templates.tpz Godot_v${GODOT_VERSION}-stable_mono_linux_x86_64.zip \
    && rm -rf templates godot_dir

# Godot pck tool
ENV PCK_TOOL_VERSION "v2.1"

RUN wget https://github.com/hhyyrylainen/GodotPckTool/releases/download/${PCK_TOOL_VERSION}/godotpcktool -O /usr/bin/godotpcktool && \
    chmod +x /usr/bin/godotpcktool

# Start of the sample project showing the issue
RUN git clone https://github.com/Revolutionary-Games/Thrive.git /Thrive

RUN cd /Thrive && git checkout 5bd14292c3000b66cf46d83e881eebc3a53c4a00

RUN cd /Thrive && git submodule update --init

RUN cd /Thrive && dotnet run --project Scripts -- native Fetch Install --debug false

# Uncomment the following line to stop the problem with getting stuck:
# RUN rm -rf /Thrive/assets/fonts

# Or uncomment the following line, which also seems to fix the problem:
# RUN cd /Thrive && godot . --import --headless --quit-after 5

# This gets stuck when trying to import the fonts for exporting with Godot
RUN cd /Thrive && dotnet run --project Scripts -- package --dehydrated Linux

```

I included the two workarounds as well which can be uncommented to see how the podman build doesn't get stuck with those workarounds enabled.

For me running that Dockerfile always ends with it being stuck like this:
```
	reimport: step 39: NotoSansSinhala-Black.ttf
	reimport: step 40: NotoSansHebrew-SemiBold.ttf
	reimport: step 41: NotoSansHebrew-Regular.ttf
	reimport: step 42: NotoSansHebrew-Light.ttf
	reimport: step 43: NotoSansHebrew-Bold.ttf
	reimport: step 44: NotoSansHebrew-Black.ttf
	reimport: step 45: NotoSansGeorgian-SemiBold.ttf
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

The Dockerfile I included demonstrates the problem when it is built. 
The font files are pretty big so even just including those in a test project might make it too big to upload.

summary: Resource folding causes Resources to be modified even if loaded with `CACHE_MODE_IGNORE`

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4

### System information

W10

### Issue description

When you load a Resource in editor with CACHE_MODE_IGNORE, it will cause Resource folding to get loaded via loaded callback:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/134da374975114ef8e05cf81d1d30eff645e310e/editor/editor_node.cpp#L6706-L6708
Which marks the Resource as edited (the original one, not the one you just loaded):
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/134da374975114ef8e05cf81d1d30eff645e310e/core/object/object.cpp#L1657-L1658
Which makes it subject to external save:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/134da374975114ef8e05cf81d1d30eff645e310e/editor/editor_node.cpp#L1851-L1854

While it's obvious that it can cause excessive saving, there is a particularly fun case where if you unfold a section, it will cause perpetual saving of that Resource. It happens with scripts:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/134da374975114ef8e05cf81d1d30eff645e310e/editor/plugins/script_editor_plugin.cpp#L2862-L2866
If Script resource has any section unfolded, it will be marked as edited when folding is loaded, which causes reload dialog to popup (bug) and when you chose to reload the script, the folding gets loaded again, marking the Script as edited and triggering another save etc.

This is the basic principle behind #103236

Ideally folding should not be handled by Object, because it leads to obscure behavior like described above.
Or at least loading folding should not mark the Resource as modified, because it makes no sense.

### Steps to reproduce

See description.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: `ParticleProcessMaterial.inherit_velocity_ratio` causes particles to be emitted from the wrong location.

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee]

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Arch Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 27 Feb 2025 18:09:44 +0000 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (nvidia; 570.124.04) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800X 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

When using a `GPUParticle2D` with a `ParticleProcessMaterial` with `inherit_velocity_ratio = 1.0`, the particles will emit 1 frame ahead of the emitter. The amount of error is affected by the `GPUParticle2D#fixed_fps` value. It gets lower as the value is increased up to the physics tick rate, but even when they are both equal there is still a visible difference.

This was previously reported using 3D particle systems here: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/97160

Sharing this to confirm it also exists in 2D.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create some moveable Node2D (I used CharacterBody2D)
2. Create new GPUParticles2D as a child.
3. Add a ParticleProcessMaterial to the GPUParticles2D
4. Set all velocity and accelerations to 0, except `inherit_velocity_ratio` which should be set to 1. Now the particle should be static relative to the emitter.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[particle-repro.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19102806/particle-repro.zip)

Instructions:
Press space to fire a projectile across the screen from the X

On the projectile there is a 2px wide black line to illustrate the x-position that the particle should emit from.
The particle is a single white square with trails enabled. You can see that the trail starts ahead of the black line.
Adjust the fixed_fps value to see the error margin change. Lower fps = more error. Any value >= to the physics tick rate has the same error.

summary: Improve error message when trying to load scene as an animation library

description.text: I was alerted to this usability issue here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jcLc5SJOR7s&t=445s Basically, we offer 3D model files as selectable items in the menu to load animation libraries, but this is only relevant for when a 3D model is imported as an AnimationLibrary. This PR improves the error message to check for 3D models imported as PackedScene, and gives the user a detailed explanation about what happened and how to get the animations from that file: Additionally, I improved the message when failing to load non-scene things:

summary: Loading a scene with ResourceLoader.LoadThreadedRequest and useSubThreads=true can cause a deadlock with C# scripts

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.stable.mono, v4.4.dev7.mono, v4.4.dev4.mono, v4.3.dev3.mono, v4.3.dev2.mono
- Not reproducible in v4.3.stable.mono, v4.4.dev1.mono, 

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-8600K CPU @ 3.60GHz (6 threads)

### Issue description

Calling `ResourceLoader.LoadThreadedRequest` with the `useSubThreads` option set to `true` can cause a deadlock after subsequently calling `ResourceLoader.LoadThreadedGet`. Example code from MRP:

```cs
ResourceLoader.LoadThreadedRequest("res://scene_to_load.tscn", nameof(PackedScene), true);
// The engine will hang after this line, and become unresponsive
PackedScene sceneA = (PackedScene)ResourceLoader.LoadThreadedGet("res://scene_to_load.tscn");
```

Not all scenes seem to exhibit this behavior, and the specific layout of the nodes / class hierarchy appears to matter.

Polling the load request with `ResourceLoader.LoadThreadedGetStatus` (instead of calling LoadThreadedGet), consistently returns `InProgress` with a progress value of `0.0`.

Debugging the project in Visual Studio and breaking all after the project freezes, then viewing the threads in the `Parallel Stacks` window shows 2 threads that have called `ScriptManagerBridge.GetOrCreateScriptBridgeForPath` that never resolve.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f95cce9-3691-4674-afad-7eb86eb1dd2c)

### Steps to reproduce

Open and run the MRP. Click the Load button, and the engine should deadlock and become unresponsive.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[deadlock-mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19101009/deadlock-mrp.zip)

summary: BaseMaterial3D Fixed Size draws shadows at an incorrect size

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.0.stable, 4.4.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable (4c311cbee) - Fedora Linux 41 (KDE Plasma) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 (nvidia; 570.86.16) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900K (32 threads)

### Issue description

Enabling BaseMaterial3D Fixed Size causes shadow drawing to be done at an incorrect size, which puts most of the scene in shadow even for a relatively small object. This can be reproduced on all rendering methods.

On the first screenshot, the red sphere on the left uses Fixed Size. The green sphere on the right never uses Fixed Size:

Fixed Size | No Fixed Size | Fixed Size, Cast Shadow = Off
-|-|-
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cbc20876-f9c9-40e5-adcd-c9352c944f71) | ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/69b8729b-ea40-4386-94c8-c5cd3797a894) | ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/958ad7c3-3767-4255-8cf1-76580691f49a)

If we use the Display PSSM Splits debug draw mode, we can see what's going on with the sphere being drawn at a fixed size in each split:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dca064dd-8a6f-438c-b384-39c23461fcd4)

OmniLight3D and SpotLight3D are also affected by this issue. For OmniLight3D, this causes shadow cubemap seams to be visible at certain light positions:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f18669f0-0b8a-48b5-90c8-b5654bdd8092

This should likely be resolved by detecting whether we are in a shadow pass in the fixed size shader code, and scaling it relative to the shadow perspective instead of the camera perspective. See https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/4443.

### Steps to reproduce

- Enable **Fixed Size** on a BaseMaterial3D that casts shadows (this means it must have an opaque material).

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

summary: Memory leak when resizing window with stretch enabled in SubViewportContainer

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Tested on: Arch Linux with Wayland, GNOME 47.

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Arch Linux ZEN SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 27 Feb 2025 18:09:27 +0000 - Wayland - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6800 XT (RADV NAVI21) - AMD Ryzen 7 7700X 8-Core Processor (16 Threads)

### Issue description

When the `Stretch` option is enabled in the `SubViewportContainer`, resizing the game window to be narrow causes a memory leak. As you make the window progressively thinner, system memory usage increases drastically.

I tested it on Linux, and the memory leak happens in editor play mode, and with exported Linux/Windows* versions as well 

_* (Windows version tested using wine)_

### Steps to reproduce

 ### **(**Warning!** This might crash your system, so make sure you don't have any unsaved work open.)**  

1. Open a task manager or a tool that allows you to monitor system memory in real time.  
2. Select the `SubViewportContainer` node and make sure `Stretch` is enabled.  
3. Run the game; it will open in a window.  
4. Start narrowing the window, making it progressively thinner.  
5. At some point, you'll notice a drastic increase in system memory usage.
6. Stop the game.  
7. Disable `Stretch` in `SubViewportContainer` and repeat steps 3 and 4.  
8. You should now see that no memory leak occurs.

summary: Add automatic C# build on editor focus

description.text:
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/2920

This PR aims to improve the usability of C# with an external IDE by automatically building the Mono project when Godot is focused and only if the CS files have changed. A new editor setting called `build_on_focus` which is disabled by default allows you toggle this feature on or off:

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/973b9198-37a0-41e1-9fe3-1f93eb22b432)

Thanks to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/93431 which also had to solve the problem of detecting when CS files have changed, I was able to repurpose a snippet of that code and hook into the existing `HotReloadAssemblyWatcher` which can detect when the Godot Editor is focused in order to build the project at the correct time. Here's that in action:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9baabafa-9903-4356-a29a-cfdfc16c2c2a

Another idea that could stem off of this would be automatically building the C# project when saving through the built in script editor, but that could get more annoying if you make changes to a lot of files, so if we were to tackle it, I believe it should be its own editor setting and its own PR. Plus, it seems like many C# users prefer to work with an IDE like Visual Studio and this functionality seems more relevant for them especially since its a familiar feature to other game engines.

Let me know if it all looks good, thanks!

summary: Lerping Camera3D towards a moving Rigidbody3D causes intense jitter when not in Exclusive Fullscreen mode

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.4.stable, 4.3.stable, 4.2.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7270) - AMD Ryzen 5 5600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

I'm trying to get a Camera3D to smoothly follow a moving Rigidbody3D. To do that I'm running `Vector3.lerp()` in the camera's `_process()` function. But when I run the game, the faster the rigidbody moves the worse the camera jitters.

Here's a video showcasing the problem:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/17335fff-d88a-4d8d-99b9-aab268f66f3b

Here are some things that _don't_ fix it:
- Changing just about any physics setting, including:
  - Physics engine (Jolt or Default)
  - Run on Separate Thread
  - Physics Ticks per Second
  - Physics Jitter Fix
  - Physics Interpolation
- Using a framerate-independent lerp with the weight being `1 - exp(speed * delta)`
- Using `get_global_transform_interpolated().origin` instead of `global_position`
- Settings the `Physics Interpolation` property of either Node
- Updating my graphics drivers

The only things that _do_ fix it are:
- Changing the camera to move in `_physics_process()` instead of `_process()` (obviously not ideal)
- Setting the window mode to Exclusive Fullscreen

I feel a little crazy because I can't really find anyone talking about this problem, even though it's so easy to reproduce and is a pretty common thing that you would want to do. Perhaps it only affects a few hardware configurations? I only found a single [Reddit comment](https://www.reddit.com/r/godot/comments/1e78h2s/comment/ldzw3dk/?utm_source=share&utm_medium=web3x&utm_name=web3xcss&utm_term=1&utm_content=share_button) from 8 months ago talking about the solution.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Make a new project
2. Add a Rigidbody3D with a MeshInstance3D and a CollisionShape3D, and set cubes for their mesh/shape respectively
3. Make a Camera3D and add a script to it with the following code
```gdscript
extends Node3D
@export var follow_target: Node3D
func _process(delta):
	global_position = global_position.lerp(follow_target.global_position + Vector3(0, 0, 2), 0.3)
```
4. Assign the RigidBody3D to the camera's `follow_target` field.
5. Ensure that ProjectSettings/Display/Window/Mode is NOT `Exclusive Fullscreen`
6. Press play and observe the jitter

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[jitter_mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19095557/jitter_mrp.zip)

summary: Cannot change resolutions and window mode

description.text:
### Tested versions

[4.4-stable](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/releases/tag/4.4-stable)

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

With the newly-added Game Embedding feature, I noticed that I can no longer change resolutions and window mode at runtime. I fixed it with setting “Project Settings - General - Display - Window - Mode” to “Windowed”, which effectively make my game open in windowed mode on start.

I understand that this is due to some feature limits, but here is the issue: if I switch the setting mentioned above back to “Exclusive Fullscreen” (which I expect my exported game to run at) and then export the build - the exported .exe build would perform as if it is still in the Game Embedding window. Even though the Game Embedding toolbar did NOT appear, I still CANNOT change resolutions and window mode.

After further testing, I discovered the following:
-This also happened in editor, even after I unchecked "Embed Game on Next Play".
-This only happened when I set Viewport Width and Height to exactly 1920*1080 in Project Settings.

Testing results:
- Editor play with “Exclusive Fullscreen” Window Mode and “Embed Game on Next Play” **checked** - cannot change resolution and the screen flickered when I tried
- Editor play with “Exclusive Fullscreen” Window Mode and “Embed Game on Next Play” **unchecked** - cannot change resolution and the screen flickered when I tried
- Editor play with “Windowed” Window Mode and “Embed Game on Next Play” **checked** - cannot change resolution and the screen flickered when I tried
- Editor play with “Windowed” Window Mode and “Embed Game on Next Play” **unchecked** - worked just fine
- Export with “Exclusive Fullscreen” Window Mode - cannot change resolution and the screen flickered when I tried
- Export with “Windowed” Window Mode - worked just fine

All these flickering behavior mentioned above looked the same, so I came to the conclusion that the Game Embed feature must be affecting my export in some weird ways.

Possible related issue: [#103171](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/103171)

### Steps to reproduce

Export the project and click on the check button.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19093409/test.zip)

summary: Gridmap error spam when regenerating MeshLibrary

description.text:
### Tested versions

I've had this issue in 4.4. and 4.3. I think I remember it happening even earlier, but I haven't recently tested any earlier versions.

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 11 - Vulkan (Forward+)

### Issue description

If you do `Scene > Export As > MeshLibrary...` and overwrite a mesh library currently being used by a gridmap in an open scene, then you get a bunch of this error: 

`ERROR: servers/rendering/renderer_rd/storage_rd/mesh_storage.cpp:667 - Parameter "mesh" is null.`

If you check "Merge with existing" then you get one of this error, but if don't check it, you get the error for at least as many meshes as are in the library. This can be quite annoying when using gridmaps with a couple dozen different tiles. To be clear, the errors don't cause any actual issue with exporting the mesh library or using the gridmap.

### Steps to reproduce

1. In the MRP, make sure the `example` scene and `grid_tiles` scene are open. 
2. In the `grid_tiles` scene, go to the top-left and do `Scene > Export As > MeshLibrary...` Overwrite the already existing `grid_tiles.meshlib`. 

If you don't check the "Merge with Existing" box, then you should get 6 of the provided error. If you do check it, you should only get 1.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[gridmap_error_spam.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19091115/gridmap_error_spam.zip)
